demographics.living_situation: lives alone in rented housing in a neighbourhood next to an industrial area; says they cannot afford to move away

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't stop coughing, my skin is burning, and the chemical smell outside is suffocating me tonight.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on hard this evening after the smell outside got really strong again, and my puffer isn't helping like it usually does.

symptom_narrative.onset: the cough and chest tightness flared up badly this evening; the patient links it to a strong chemical smell and increased flaring from the nearby industrial site over the last week
symptom_narrative.duration: acutely worse over the last few hours tonight, on a background of months to years of on-and-off breathing irritation
symptom_narrative.character: a constant hacking cough, a tight band across the chest, and a whistling sound when breathing out; skin feels like it is burning and intensely itchy
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says it is the worst the breathing has been, feels like they can't get a full breath and are working hard just to breathe
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest and throat for the breathing; forearms, neck and face for the burning rash
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse tonight and worse whenever the outdoor smell is strong; has been building over the past 7 days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much tonight — the blue reliever puffer usually helps but tonight it is barely touching it even after using it again and again; running air purifiers indoors helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the chemical smell from outside, going near a window, and coughing fits
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling very anxious and on edge, frightened about how unsafe the local air and water feel, exhausted from not being able to breathe easily
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: has had recurring bouts of cough, chest tightness and skin irritation over the past few years that the patient believes are tied to living near the industrial area, but says no one has ever fully explained it
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot settle, cannot do anything except sit upright and try to breathe; too breathless and itchy to sleep

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: recurring breathing trouble and skin flare-ups over several years that the patient links to their neighbourhood's air quality
past_medical_history.how_managed: uses a blue reliever puffer when the breathing gets bad; no clear ongoing plan

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: used it about 6 times in the last 2 hours tonight with barely any relief

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives right next to an industrial area and cannot afford to relocate; there has been more industrial flaring and a strong chemical odour over the past week; keeps the windows taped shut and runs commercial air purifiers at maximum; feels ignored and let down by local authorities about the air and water safety

access_barriers.financial: cannot afford to relocate away from the industrial exposure
access_barriers.other: distrust of municipal health authorities over unaddressed environmental concerns

objective_data_offered.value: sitting upright, breathing fast and visibly working hard to breathe, coughing frequently
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: impression over a video call; degree of respiratory effort and air entry cannot be measured or auscultated remotely

objective_data_offered.value: I can hear myself wheezing / whistling when I breathe out
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported sound, not an auscultated finding

objective_data_offered.value: widespread red, raw, scratched patches on both forearms, the neck and the face
a strong industrial chemical smell in and around the home; windows taped shut; air purifiers running at maximum
